stage2.narrative:
In 2024, state regulators suspended new intakes at Echo Glen Children's Center after overcrowding reached a level described as compromising the safety of staff and young people, with the facility's population at its total capacity of 112. That same year, multiple escapes occurred at the facility, including a notable May 2024 incident involving seven students. An investigation by a local news outlet revealed that administrators had been warned of the planned escape days before it took place, but requests for additional staffing were denied and security measures remained inadequate.

Also in 2024, law firms began actively investigating allegations of sexual assault at Echo Glen Children's Center. Reports indicate the facility has a documented history of abuse reflected in civil lawsuits, public settlements, and criminal investigations.

At the district level, two former students sued the Issaquah School District in 2018, alleging the district failed to protect them following a sexual assault and that they endured years of bullying and harassment after the incident was reported. The lawsuit alleged that a superintendent, a principal, and a school officer failed to address repeated complaints. No resolution is noted in the available record.

Separately, a former employee of Endeavour Elementary School filed a lawsuit against the Issaquah School District in an undated filing, claiming district leaders disregarded complaints that a principal repeatedly sexually harassed the employee and mistreated students. No resolution is noted in the available record.

In an undated report from January 2026, parents and community members submitted a petition calling for an independent investigation into district leadership's handling of student and staff safety and Title IX compliance. The petition alleged that administrators dismissed credible reports of threats, aggression, assault, drugs, and weapons on campus, and failed to enforce applicable federal and state mandates regarding emergency removals. No resolution is noted in the available record.
